evals.prompt: I have a Node.js Express API project in ~/projects/my-api. I want to start using Claude Code with it properly. Set it up correctly.
evals.expected_output: Creates .claude/ directory with settings.json (permissions + hooks), creates CLAUDE.md with all 5 required sections (overview, build/test commands, architecture, conventions, notes), adds proper .gitignore entries (.claude/settings.local.json, .claude/worktrees/), and reports a summary of what was created vs skipped. Does NOT overwrite existing files without asking.

evals.prompt: This is a Claude Code plugin I'm building. Can you audit it and fix anything that doesn't comply with the Build with Claude Code spec? The plugin has some agents and hooks already.
evals.expected_output: Runs Phase 1 DETECT commands, identifies .claude-plugin/ as plugin project, reads spec-checklist.md to audit all 9+ checklist sections, identifies missing items (e.g., missing <example> blocks in agent descriptions, wrong hooks.json format, missing ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} in paths), reports PRESENT/MISSING/NEEDS REVIEW for each item, and creates/fixes missing files. Invokes agent-development and hook-development sub-skills as needed.

evals.prompt: Add Claude Code structure to my project so I can use agents and hooks. I want a code reviewer agent and a pre-commit hook.
evals.expected_output: Scaffolds .claude/agents/ and .claude/hooks/ directories, invokes agent-development skill to create code-reviewer.md with proper <example> blocks, invokes hook-development skill to create PreToolUse hook in .claude/settings.json, generates a settings.json with correct permissions structure, reports completion summary with next steps.

evals.prompt: Convert my monorepo to follow Claude Code specs. It has Python and TypeScript packages in packages/*. Make sure the MCP config is also set up.
evals.expected_output: Detects regular project (no .claude-plugin/), creates .claude/ structure, creates CLAUDE.md with monorepo architecture section documenting packages/, creates .mcp.json using template from references/templates.md with placeholder stdio and http servers, runs validation checks (jq on JSON files, line count on CLAUDE.md), invokes mcp-integration skill for server configuration details, outputs final compliance report.

evals.prompt: Audit my project for Claude Code compliance. Here's what I have: a CLAUDE.md (120 lines), a .claude/settings.json with just {} in it, and nothing else.
evals.expected_output: Runs DETECT phase, identifies as regular project. Reads spec-checklist.md. Reports CLAUDE.md as present but flags unknown section coverage. Reports settings.json as NEEDS REVIEW (missing permissions and hooks keys). Scaffolds missing .claude/ subdirs (.rules, .skills, .agents, .hooks, .output-styles). Outputs minimal settings.json template with correct permission_mode: 'default'. Does NOT overwrite existing CLAUDE.md. Outputs ✓/✗/~ status for every checklist item.
